themes.title: New capital pools fueling growth
themes.detail: Retail, wealth, retirement and insurance channels are the dominant source of fresh AUM — cited as a top growth driver by BlackRock, Amundi, Apollo, Blackstone, KKR and others.

themes.title: Wealth distribution is being productized
themes.detail: Managers are building private-market access products (model portfolios, interval funds, SMAs, evergreen structures) aimed at the RIA / wealth channel. Global Wealth AUM was highlighted as a step-function growth engine at Apollo, KKR and Blackstone.

themes.title: Private Credit broadens beyond direct lending
themes.detail: Asset-backed, infrastructure credit and investment-grade private credit expanding rapidly; GS, Apollo, Blackstone, KKR and Carlyle all announced large origination / insurance-linked partnerships.

themes.title: AI infrastructure as a new capex super-cycle
themes.detail: Data centers, power, digital infrastructure is attracting dedicated vehicles. Blackstone, Brookfield, KKR and BlackRock (GIP) all highlighted AI-infra as a multi-year demand signal.

themes.title: Tokenization + AI productivity step-change
themes.detail: Managers broadly reference tokenized funds, blockchain settlement, and AI-driven operating leverage; BlackRock, State Street and JPMorgan are leaning in hardest on the tech stack side.

initiatives: Close on GIP integration; scale private-markets platform (infra, credit, private equity)
Expand Aladdin + eFront unified tech stack (incl. Preqin private-data)
Push model portfolios and SMAs in US wealth channel

cost_pressures: Acquisition-related expense (GIP, Preqin, HPS) pressuring operating margin
Comp ratio elevated from talent integration
strategic_insights: Whole-portfolio offering: public + private + tech is a key differentiator
Organic base fee growth trending at the high end of the 5%+ target
Leaning into retirement + wealth as the largest structural demand pools

clients.theme: BIF V infra close + transition capital

qualitative_insights.competitive_dynamics: Scale players (BlackRock, Blackstone, Apollo) are widening the gap via private-markets platforms; mid-size traditional managers face margin compression.
qualitative_insights.strategic_partnerships: Insurance / reinsurance tie-ups (Apollo-Athene, KKR-Global Atlantic, Brookfield Wealth Solutions) are generating durable permanent capital.
qualitative_insights.feature_requests: Clients across cohort continue asking MSCI for deeper private-markets data, tokenized / alternatives analytics, and integrated climate-risk feeds in Aladdin/eFront-style workflows.
qualitative_insights.regulatory_pricing_pressure: Index fee compression in passive; regulatory capital and SEC private-funds rules pressuring alt-manager operating cost bases.
